decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85.0°C limit. However, the optimization history indicates diminishing returns from geometry changes and suggests shifting to material tuning. Since the spreader conductivity is already at its upper bound (500.0), the next most effective lever is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve heat extraction towards the Dirichlet boundaries.

changes.reason: Doubling the base plate conductivity (within the allowed 2.0x ratio) enhances lateral and vertical heat spreading in the lower domain, reducing thermal resistance to the cooled side walls.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution in the base plate
Improved heat flux towards the left and right boundaries
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts entirely to the interface or spreader limits
Further increases in base conductivity may yield smaller gains compared to initial jumps
